messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "distributor-line-review-lockout",
 "title": "Annual line reviews plus Legrand's dual-brand grip close the distributor cell mid-cycle — the entrant arrives with the shelf already allocated",
 "narrative": "The 0.13 distributor cell looks like the easy channel: no framework agreements, documented master-distributor models, even explicit private-label options for distributors in buyer's guides. But distribution in this category runs on annual line reviews with exclusivity and margin-program terms, and the intelligent-PDU shelf specifically is consolidated — Legrand owns BOTH Raritan and Server Technology, the two intelligent-PDU specialist brands, giving one vendor two slots on every line card and strong leverage in review negotiations. A pre-launch entrant hits the market mid-cycle: even a willing distributor cannot onboard a conflicting line until the next review, which lands at or after the end of the venture's Year-1 window. The cell is not small — for the YAM horizon it is closed.",
 "category": "competitive",
 "targetNodes": [
  "cust.distributor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Master-distributor structures (the MaxPower pattern: one distributor supplying all reseller partners) concentrate line-card decisions into few annual review events, and Legrand's ownership of both Raritan and Server Technology means the intelligent-PDU slots on those line cards are already double-occupied by one incumbent",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c7",
    "digest:cr3-vendors#c4",
    "ledger:cust.distributor"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The venture launches mid-review-cycle (profile: pre-launch, first 12 months); the documented private-label route exists but requires certifications, stock commitments and margin programs a Year-1 entrant cannot yet fund, so no CE distributor can list the product inside the YAM window",
   "refs": [
    "profile:Stage",
    "digest:cust-mix#c13",
    "ledger:cust.distributor"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "cust.distributor (0.13, maturity needs-source, zero corroborating evidence in the ledger) contributes nothing to Year-1 reachable demand; the funnel silently overstates the channel mix available inside the obtainable window",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:cust.distributor",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "Distribution looks like the LOW-commitment channel — 'anyone can get listed eventually' — and the ledger's 0.13 looks too small to interrogate. The hidden variable is timing: line reviews are annual gates, so a cell that is genuinely open in steady state is closed for precisely the 12-month window the YAM measures. Founders model channel share, not channel calendar.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "A named CE electrical/IT distributor (Rexel, Sonepar, Also-class) confirming in writing that a new intelligent-PDU line can be onboarded within two quarters without waiting for the annual line review or triggering an exclusivity conflict.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.45,
  "rationale": "Annual line reviews with incumbency advantages are the documented norm in electrical distribution (base-rate), and the Legrand dual-brand consolidation of the intelligent-PDU shelf is a hard fact; the open question is how rigidly CE distributors enforce review timing for a differentiated product.",
  "basis": "base-rate"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "cust.distributor",
   "op": "exclude",
   "value": null,
   "note": "Distributor cell unavailable to the venture within the Year-1 horizon — foreclosed by review-cycle timing and shelf exclusivity, not shrunk"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Intelligent-PDU brands listed on CE distributor line cards and webshops",
   "where": "Rexel Germany / Sonepar online catalogs; Also and Ingram Micro DC-infrastructure category pages",
   "threshold": "Raritan + Server Technology + APC occupying all intelligent-PDU listings with no non-incumbent brand added over two quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Legrand channel-program announcements tightening intelligent-PDU distribution terms",
   "where": "Legrand investor communications and EMEA partner-program pages; distribution trade press (ChannelPartner.de)",
   "threshold": "Any announced exclusivity, rebate-tier or dual-brand bundling program covering Raritan/Server Technology in EMEA",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission a channel audit: map line-review dates, exclusivity terms and private-label requirements across the top 5 CE electrical/IT distributors",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "cust.distributor",
   "note": "Turns an assumed 0.13 cell into a dated calendar of when (if ever) it opens"
  },
  {
   "action": "Enter via a distributor's private-label program (documented option) rather than as a branded line — sidesteps the brand-slot conflict",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Buyer's-guide evidence shows distributors actively seek OEM/private-label intelligent-PDU options"
  },
  {
   "action": "Time distributor outreach to the review calendar and pre-book slots for Year-2 rather than burning Year-1 sales capacity on a closed gate",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "competitive-foreclosure",
 "riskId": "risk.competitive-foreclosure.distributor-line-review-lockout"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

Also and Ingram Micro DC-infrastructure category pages","threshold":"Raritan + Server Technology + APC occupying all intelligent-PDU listings with no non-incumbent brand added over two quarters","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Legrand channel-program announcements tightening intelligent-PDU distribution terms","where":"Legrand investor communications and EMEA partner-program pages; distribution trade press (ChannelPartner.de)","threshold":"Any announced exclusivity, rebate-tier or dual-brand bundling program covering Raritan/Server Technology in EMEA","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission a channel audit: map line-review dates, exclusivity terms and private-label requirements across the top 5 CE electrical/IT distributors","type":"information","voiNodeId":"cust.distributor","note":"Turns an assumed 0.13 cell into a dated calendar of when (if ever) it opens"},{"action":"Enter via a distributor's private-label program (documented option) rather than as a branded line — sidesteps the brand-slot conflict","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Buyer's-guide evidence shows distributors actively seek OEM/private-label intelligent-PDU options"},{"action":"Time distributor outreach to the review calendar and pre-book slots for Year-2 rather than burning Year-1 sales capacity on a closed gate","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":null}]}
